visible_agent_inputs.question: How old was the man who coached the the Kansas Jayhawks during their second season ever in 1891?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1: Passage [1] identifies the coach as "English professor and head football coach Edwin Mortimer Hopkins" for the 1891 Kansas Jayhawks football team (quote: "In their first and only season under English professor and head football coach Edwin Mortimer Hopkins"). Missing fact: Hopkins' age in 1891 — requires Edwin Mortimer Hopkins' birth date or age. Also note the passages do not state that 1891 was the program's "second season ever"; if the question depends on that phrasing, next hop should verify the team's inaugural season (e.g., check "1890 Kansas Jayhawks football team" or "first season Kansas Jayhawks football"). Retrieval anchors/queries: "Edwin Mortimer Hopkins birth date", "Edwin M. Hopkins born", "Edwin Mortimer Hopkins biography", and "1890 Kansas Jayhawks football" (or "Kansas Jayhawks first season").

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Edwin Mortimer Hopkins | Edwin Mortimer "E. M." Hopkins (September 16, 1862 – June 13, 1946) was a university professor and American football coach. He served on the faculty at the University of Kansas from 1891 to 1937, where he was the head of the English department for many years. Hopkins graduated from Princeton University in 1888 with a bachelor's degree in English and earned a master's degree the following year. While at Princeton he was elected to the "Lit" board in 1888. That same year h ...[truncated]

1891 Kansas Jayhawks football team | The 1891 Kansas Jayhawks football team represented the University of Kansas as an independent during the 1891 college football season. In their first and only season under English professor and head football coach Edwin Mortimer Hopkins, the Jayhawks compiled a 7–0–1 record and outscored opponents by a combined total of 160 to 54. The season included the first football games played against both Iowa and Missouri. John Kenzie was the team captain.

Jesse J. Guidry | Jesse J. Guidry (dates of birth and death missing; probably March 21, 1921 – September 2, 1987) was a Democratic politician from Cecilia in St. Martin Parish in South Louisiana, who served from 1973 to 1981 as a member of the Louisiana House of Representatives during the first two administrations of Governor Edwin Washington Edwards.

William Hopkins Holyland | William Hopkins Holyland (born 1807) was an English accountant. He is best known for having co-founded, with Samuel Lowell Price and Edwin Waterhouse, the accountancy practice of "Price Waterhouse" that now forms part of PriceWaterhouseCoopers.

Edwin O. Reischauer Center for East Asian Studies | Edwin O. Reischauer Center for East Asian Studies at Johns Hopkins University is a research center within the Paul H. Nitze School of Advanced International Studies (SAIS). The center supports the research and study of transpacific and intra-Asian relations. The mission of the center is to advance mutual understanding between Northeast Asia and the United States.

Date of birth of Jesus | The date of birth of Jesus is not stated in the gospels or in any secular text, but most scholars assume a date of birth between 6 BC and 4 BC. The historical evidence is too incomplete to allow a definitive dating, but the date is estimated through two different approaches—one by analyzing references to known historical events mentioned in the nativity accounts in the Gospels of Luke and Matthew, and the second by working backwards from the estimation of the start of th ...[truncated]

source_state.answer: he was 28 for most of 1891 and turned 29 on September 16, 1891.

Senior Paul Endacott was named a consensus All-American fo ...[truncated]

1903–04 Kansas Jayhawks men's basketball team | The 1903-1904 Kansas Jayhawks men's basketball team represented the University of Kansas in its sixth season of collegiate basketball. The head coach was Dr. James Naismith, the inventor of the game, who served his 6th year.
